notes: Run 2026-07-03. 15 clues: 3×$200, 3×$400, 3×$600, 3×$800, 3×$1000. Subcats: Popular Music (6), Dance (2), Theater (5), Broadway & Musicals (1), Jazz & Blues (1).

questions.clue: Nicknamed 'Ol' Blue Eyes' and 'Chairman of the Board,' this Hoboken-born singer of Italian descent joined the Harry James band as its vocalist in 1939.

citations.title: Frank Sinatra
citations.snippet: Francis Albert Sinatra was an American singer and actor. Nicknamed the "Chairman of the Board" and "Ol' Blue Eyes", he is regarded as one of the most influential entertainers of the 20th century.

questions.clue: This high-energy music-hall dance, popular in French cabarets since the 1840s, is performed by a chorus line known for vigorous skirt manipulation and spectacular high kicks.

citations.title: Can-can

questions.clue: Formed in Boston in 1970 and nicknamed 'the Bad Boys from Boston,' this blues-based hard rock band features vocalist Steven Tyler and guitarist Joe Perry.

citations.title: Aerosmith
citations.snippet: Aerosmith is an American rock band formed in Boston in 1970. The group consists of lead vocalist Steven Tyler, bassist Tom Hamilton, drummer Joey Kramer, and guitarists Joe Perry and Brad Whitford. Their style, which is rooted in blues-base

questions.clue: Danced to big-band music in 4/4 time, this smooth, progressive ballroom dance developed in the 1910s resembles the waltz in appearance and peaked in popularity during the 1930s.

citations.title: Foxtrot
citations.snippet: The foxtrot is a smooth, progressive dance characterized by long, continuous flowing movements across the dance floor. It is danced to big band music. The dance is similar in its look to waltz, although the rhythm is in a 44 time signature

questions.clue: This Italian theatre form, popular across Europe between the 16th and 18th centuries, features masked stock characters and gave rise to celebrated actresses including Isabella Andreini.

citations.title: Commedia dell'arte

questions.clue: This Broadway actress won two Tony Awards for Best Actress in a Musical — for the title role in Thoroughly Modern Millie (2002) and for Reno Sweeney in Anything Goes (2011).

citations.title: Sutton Foster

questions.clue: Two albums with Frank Sinatra — starting with the 1962 LP subtitled 'An Historic Musical First' — brought this jazz bandleader's orchestra to a mainstream pop audience.

citations.title: Frank Sinatra
citations.snippet: Francis Albert Sinatra was an American singer and actor. Nicknamed the "Chairman of the Board" and "Ol' Blue Eyes", he is regarded as one of the most influential entertainers of the 20th century.

questions.clue: This American playwright received three Tony Awards, the Pulitzer Prize for Drama, and the Mark Twain Prize while writing more than 30 plays and nearly as many screenplays.

citations.title: Neil Simon
citations.snippet: Marvin Neil Simon was an American playwright, screenwriter and author. He wrote more than 30 plays and nearly the same number of movie screenplays, mostly film adaptations of his plays. He received three Tony Awards and a Golden Globe Award

questions.clue: Registered at the London Stationers' Company as a broadside ballad in September 1580, this traditional English melody was documented in several late 16th-century lute books.

citations.title: Greensleeves

questions.clue: Eugene O'Neill's openly autobiographical four-act play was withheld from publication; its 1956 Broadway premiere won the Tony Award for Best Play and earned him a posthumous Pulitzer.

citations.title: Long Day's Journey into Night
citations.snippet: Long Day's Journey into Night is a play in four acts written by American playwright Eugene O'Neill in 1939–1941 and first published posthumously in 1956. It is widely regarded as his magnum opus and one of the great American plays of the 20

questions.clue: Their 2004 single 'Tessie' marked this Celtic punk band's mainstream breakthrough; a 2005 track featured in a Martin Scorsese crime film then became their only platinum single.

questions.clue: Cryptic lyrics exploring UFOs and biblical violence, atypical meter signatures, and a vocal range from guttural screams to measured melody define this alternative rock guitarist's signature sound.

questions.clue: This country singer's 2021 double album was the first of its genre to spend its opening seven weeks at number one on the Billboard 200.

citations.title: Morgan Wallen
